evidence: Round 1: BLOCKED because the candidate manifest was disconnected from machine G1 authority, central/template paths still named the v1.5 G1/bundle, and a historical wrapper-hash assertion had been removed.
Round 2: NO_BLOCKER after the v1.6 manifest, verdict, Formal G1 and final-bundle paths were forward-bound and the historical/current wrapper-hash assertions were restored.

Round 3: NO_BLOCKER on commit 1d9b5dba after the still-allowlisted export template was rebound to the v1.6 G1/bundle names; the adjudicated manifest is SHA-256 c2d1338c719966700c0002050cecfa5cf586f6c3cc64866000dc38d733ac2e75.
findings.summary: The three-round authorization-path and governance-assertion blockers are resolved without changing scientific authority.

evidence: The round-3 manifest has exactly 70 sorted unique rows and independently verified 70/70 hashes.
Authority remains limited to rq014_g2_declassification_export and rq014_g2_contract_preflight; resource pilot, G2R, G3R, G4R and rating access remain denied.
The protected recovery lane, envelope contract, score-stripped schema and both config spaces remain byte-identical to the pre-authorization parent.
findings.summary: Round-3 scope, manifest integrity, rating blindness and protected science bytes are intact.

evidence: The persisted round-3 statistics report records no workspace, launcher or HPC action and no new defect.
No rating value was accessed and no HPC job was submitted during statistics review.
findings.summary: The statistics review stayed inside the rating-blind, no-execution boundary.
